label: Customer.io

tools.name: DeletePerson
tools.description:
Delete a person record to honor a data-removal request.

Deleting a person who does not exist is accepted as a no-op.

parameters.name: person_id
parameters.description: Id of the person to delete.

parameters.name: identifier_type
parameters.description: Which kind of value person_id is, so the delete targets the right profile. Defaults to the external id you assigned the person; use the Customer.io internal id when person_id is a cio_id (people with no external id are returned that way), or email when it is an email address.

tools.name: GetCampaignMetrics
tools.description: Read a campaign's aggregate delivery metrics over a period.

parameters.name: campaign_id
parameters.description: Id of the campaign whose metrics to read.

parameters.name: period
parameters.description: Granularity of the metric series. Defaults to days.

parameters.name: steps
parameters.description: Number of periods to report, counting back from now. Defaults to 30.

tools.name: GetNewsletterMetrics
tools.description: Read a newsletter's aggregate delivery metrics over a period.

parameters.name: newsletter_id
parameters.description: Id of the newsletter whose metrics to read.

parameters.name: period
parameters.description: Granularity of the metric series. Defaults to days.

parameters.name: steps
parameters.description: Number of periods to report, counting back from now. Defaults to 30.

tools.name: GetPerson
tools.description:
Look up a person's profile by email or id.

Resolves the person by whichever identifier is supplied; an email shared by
several people reports every match in the result.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email address to look up the person by. Provide this or a person id.

parameters.name: person_id
parameters.description: Customer.io person id to look up. Provide this or an email.

tools.name: IdentifyPerson
tools.description:
Create or update a person and their attributes.

Existing attributes not named in this call are preserved; named attributes
are added or overwritten.

parameters.name: person_id
parameters.description: Stable id that identifies the person; creates the person if new.

parameters.name: identifier_type
parameters.description: Which kind of value person_id is, so the write targets the right profile. Defaults to the external id you assigned the person; use the Customer.io internal id when person_id is a cio_id (people with no external id are returned that way), or email when it is an email address.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email to set on the person. Provided values are stored as the email attribute; leave empty to leave the email unchanged.

parameters.name: attributes
parameters.description: Attributes to set or update on the person, as a flat mapping of attribute name to value. Omitted attributes are left unchanged.

tools.name: ListBroadcasts
tools.description: List the workspace's broadcasts to discover ids that can be triggered.

tools.name: ListCampaigns
tools.description: List the campaigns in the workspace.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum campaigns to return (1-1000). Defaults to 50.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: 0-indexed starting position. Defaults to 0 (first result).

tools.name: ListNewsletters
tools.description: List the newsletters in the workspace.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum newsletters to return (1-1000). Defaults to 50.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: 0-indexed starting position. Defaults to 0 (first result).

tools.name: ListPersonActivities
tools.description:
List a person's recent activities, most recent first, resolved by email
or id. Optionally filter by activity type and/or name; filtering by name
requires an activity type. When an email matches several people, the first
match's activities are returned.

parameters.name: person_id
parameters.description: Customer.io person id. Provide this or an email.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email of the person. Provide this or a person id.

parameters.name: activity_type
parameters.description: Restrict to one activity type. Leave empty for all types.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Restrict to activities with this exact name (e.g. an event name). Requires an activity type to also be set. Leave empty for any name.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum activities to return (1-1000). Defaults to 50.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Pagination cursor from a prior page. Leave empty for the first page.

tools.name: ListPersonMessages
tools.description:
List a person's message and delivery history, most recent first,
resolved by email or id. When an email matches several people, the first
match's messages are returned.

parameters.name: person_id
parameters.description: Customer.io person id. Provide this or an email.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email of the person. Provide this or a person id.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum messages to return (1-1000). Defaults to 50.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Pagination cursor from a prior page. Leave empty for the first page.

tools.name: ListPersonSegments
tools.description:
List the segments a person belongs to, resolved by email or id. When an
email matches several people, the first match's segments are returned.

parameters.name: person_id
parameters.description: Customer.io person id. Provide this or an email.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email of the person. Provide this or a person id.

tools.name: ListSegmentMembers
tools.description: List the ids of the people in a segment and the segment's total size.

parameters.name: segment_id
parameters.description: Id of the segment whose members to list.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum member ids to return (1-1000). Defaults to 100.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Pagination cursor from a prior page. Leave empty for the first page.

tools.name: ListSegments
tools.description: List the segments defined in the workspace.

tools.name: ListTransactionalMessages
tools.description: List the transactional message templates in the workspace.

tools.name: RecordEvent
tools.description:
Record a named custom event on a person's timeline to drive lifecycle
journeys.

parameters.name: person_id
parameters.description: Id of the person the event is recorded against.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Name of the custom event (e.g. purchased, signed_up).

parameters.name: identifier_type
parameters.description: Which kind of value person_id is, so the event targets the right profile. Defaults to the external id you assigned the person; use the Customer.io internal id when person_id is a cio_id (people with no external id are returned that way), or email when it is an email address.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Event data as a flat mapping of property name to value. Leave empty for an event with no data.

tools.name: SearchPeople
tools.description:
Find people by segment membership and/or an attribute, to build an
audience without a known id. Supply at least one of a segment or an
attribute name; both combine with AND.

parameters.name: segment_id
parameters.description: Restrict to members of this segment. Leave empty to not filter by segment.

parameters.name: attribute_name
parameters.description: Restrict to people who have this attribute. Combine with an attribute value to match a specific value, or leave the value empty to match anyone the attribute is set on. Leave empty to not filter by attribute.

parameters.name: attribute_value
parameters.description: Value the named attribute must equal. Ignored unless an attribute name is given; leave empty to match any value the attribute is set to.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum people to return (1-1000). Defaults to 50.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Pagination cursor from a prior page. Leave empty for the first page.

tools.name: SendTransactionalEmail
tools.description: Send a transactional email to one recipient by its template id.

parameters.name: transactional_message_id
parameters.description: Id of the transactional message template to send.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Recipient email address. The send is associated with the person who has this email; provide an email address, not a person id.

parameters.name: message_data
parameters.description: Values for the template's variables, as a flat mapping of name to value. Leave empty when the template has no variables.

parameters.name: subject
parameters.description: Subject line override. Leave empty to use the template's subject.

parameters.name: from_address
parameters.description: From address override. Leave empty to use the template's sender.

parameters.name: bcc
parameters.description: BCC address. Leave empty for none.

parameters.name: reply_to
parameters.description: Reply-to address override. Leave empty to use the template's default.

tools.name: TriggerBroadcast
tools.description: Trigger an API broadcast to a segment or an explicit recipient list.

parameters.name: broadcast_id
parameters.description: Id of the API-triggered broadcast to launch.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Trigger data referenced by the broadcast's content/liquid, as a flat mapping of name to value. Leave empty when the broadcast references no data.

parameters.name: segment_id
parameters.description: Segment id to send to. Leave empty to use the broadcast's configured audience or an explicit recipient list.

parameters.name: recipient_ids
parameters.description: Explicit external customer ids to send to (the id you assigned the person, not a Customer.io cio_id). Leave empty to target a segment, recipient emails, or the configured audience.

parameters.name: recipient_emails
parameters.description: Explicit recipient email addresses to send to. Prefer this when you only have an email or a cio_id. Leave empty to target a segment or ids.

summary:
The Customer.io toolkit lets LLMs interact with a Customer.io workspace via the Tracking and App APIs. It covers the full lifecycle of contacts, messaging, and analytics — from identifying people and recording events through sending transactional emails and triggering broadcasts.

## Capabilities

- **Contact management**: Create, update, look up, search, and delete person profiles; resolve by email or ID; handle multi-match edge cases.
- **Activity & message history**: Retrieve a person's timeline of activities (filterable by type/name) and full message/delivery history.
- **Segments**: List workspace segments, check which segments a person belongs to, and enumerate segment members.
- **Campaigns, newsletters & broadcasts**: List campaigns, newsletters, and broadcasts; read aggregate delivery metrics for campaigns and newsletters; trigger API broadcasts to segments or explicit recipient lists.
- **Transactional messaging**: List transactional message templates and send transactional emails to a single recipient by template ID.
- **Event tracking**: Record named custom events on a person's timeline to drive lifecycle journeys.

## Secrets

This toolkit uses no OAuth flow. All credentials are supplied as secrets.

- **`CUSTOMERIO_SITE_ID`** — The Site ID for your Customer.io workspace, used to authenticate Tracking API calls. Find it in the Customer.io dashboard under **Settings → API Credentials → Tracking API**. [Customer.io API Credentials docs](https://customer.io/docs/api/track/#section/Authentication)

- **`CUSTOMERIO_TRACKING_API_KEY`** — The Tracking API Key paired with the Site ID above. Retrieved from the same **Settings → API Credentials → Tracking API** page. This key authenticates event, identify, and delete calls sent to the Tracking API endpoint.

- **`CUSTOMERIO_APP_API_KEY`** — A Bearer token for the Customer.io App API (used for reading campaigns, newsletters, segments, sending transactional email, triggering broadcasts, etc.). Generate one in **Settings → API Credentials → App API Keys**. Scope the key to the minimum permissions your use case requires. [Customer.io App API auth docs](https://customer.io/docs/api/app/#section/Authentication)

- **`CUSTOMERIO_REGION`** — Specifies the data region for your workspace (`US` or `EU`). This determines which API base URLs are used. You can confirm your region in **Settings → Workspace Settings**. Provide the appropriate string so requests are routed to the correct regional endpoint.

See the [Arcade secrets guide](https://docs.arcade.dev/en/guides/create-tools/tool-basics/create-tool-secrets) for how to configure secrets, and manage them at [https://api.arcade.dev/dashboard/auth/secrets](https://api.arcade.dev/dashboard/auth/secrets).
